title: Asian shares mostly higher tracking Wall Street gains and oil stabilizes
description: Asian shares are mostly higher tracking Wall Street gains, with South Korea's stock market, rebounding after earlier sharp losses on a tech sell-off. U.S. futures have edged up. South Korea's benchmark Kospi index gained 1.3%, while Japan's Nikkei 225 was up 0.9%. Oil prices are stabilizing after the U.S. and Iran said separately they would be sending delegations to Qatar, although Iran has denied that talks have been arranged with the United States. Brent crude, the international standard, was trading 0.2% lower at above $73 a barrel, slightly higher than pre-war levels. On Monday, Wall Street's benchmark S&P 500 added 1.2%. The Dow Jones Industrial Average climbed 0.6%, and the Nasdaq composite gained 2.1%.

title: Asian stocks are mixed after big tech sell-off
description: Asian stocks are mixed on Wednesday, following a sell-off in big technology stocks from Asia to Wall Street. U.S. stock futures are also trading mixed. Global investors are monitoring market movements in Japan and South Korea, which have seen big gains in recent months on the global AI boom but both fell sharply on Tuesday. South Korea's benchmark Kospi index was up 0.5% on Wednesday, while Japan's Nikkei 225 lost 1.1%. On Wall Street, the benchmark S&P 500 fell 1.4% Tuesday. The tech-heavy Nasdaq composite dropped 2.2%, while the Dow was 0.1% lower. Oil prices fell as more ships crossed the Strait of Hormuz and as U.S.-Iran talks on a permanent end to the Iran war progressed.
